title: magnolia × gotoburgensis
intro: Magnolia × gotoburgensis, also known as the Southern Magnolia, is a deciduous evergreen tree hybrid that is native to the southeastern United States. The bark, leaves, and flowers of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, with its medicinal properties attributed to its high content of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Magnolia × gotoburgensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its extracts are also used in modern pharmaceutical products.
